'''Tulsen Paul Tollett''' (born 6 May 1973) is a television presenter and former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s and 2000s. A [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] international representative utility back, he played in Australia for Sydney's [[Penrith Panthers]] and the [[Parramatta Eels]] clubs, before moving to England and playing for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]].

==Background==
Tollett was born in [[Hastings]], Sussex in the United Kingdom, but moved with his family to Australia as a youngster. His junior career was with Emu Plains JRLC prior to being signed by Penrith.<ref name=TakeToll/>

==Rugby league career==
Tollett played at the Penrith Panthers and the Parramatta Eels<ref name=TakeToll/> before joining the Broncos for the 1996 inaugural season of [[Super League I]].<ref name=CallsDay>[http://www.walesonline.co.uk/rugbynation/rugby-news/2001/11/30/broncos-tollett-calls-it-a-day-91466-11453942/ Broncos' Tollett calls it a day] Wales Online, 30 November 2001</ref> He played his junior football for [[Emu Plains JRLFC|Emu Plains]].

After a successful first season in England, he was selected for the [[Great Britain national rugby league team|Great Britain]] squad to tour Papua New Guinea, Fiji and New Zealand.<ref name=CallsDay/>

Tollett's highlights with the Broncos include:

*Playing in the side that beat top Australian side [[Canberra Raiders]] in Super League's World Club Championship series in 1997 (London Broncos chalked up a rare win by a Super League side against their [[Super League (Australia)|Australasian Super League]] counterparts in that year's competition)
*Being part of the first London Broncos side to reach a Rugby League [[Challenge Cup]] final, in 1999 against the [[Leeds Rhinos]] at [[Wembley Stadium (1923)|Wembley]]<ref name=TakeToll>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1683127.stm Injury takes toll on Tollett] BBC Sport, 29 November 2001</ref>
*Being in the highest finishing London side (second, Super League 1997) in the club's ([[London Broncos|Fulham, London Crusaders, Broncos]]) history to date

==Rugby union career==
Tollett also had spells in [[rugby union]] with English Premiership side [[Harlequin F.C.|Harlequins]] during 1997/98,<ref>[http://www.quins.co.uk/rugby/harlequins_rugby_union_1st_xv.php?player=8547&includeref=dynamic Tulsen Tollett] {{webarchive|url=https://archive.today/20130421071658/http://www.quins.co.uk/rugby/harlequins_rugby_union_1st_xv.php?player=8547&includeref=dynamic |date=21 April 2013}} Harlequins Rugby Union</ref> as well as [[London Welsh]] in 1999/2000 during the Rugby League close season.<ref>[http://www.worcesternews.co.uk/archive/2003/07/17/Worcestershire+Archive/7625070.London_Welsh_19_Worcester_20/ London Welsh 19 Worcester 20] Worcester News, 2 October 1999</ref>

==Media work==
Tollett was forced to give up playing in 2001 due to a persistent shoulder injury.<ref>[https://www.telegraph.co.uk/sport/rugbyleague/3017807/Barrow-sign-up-Nickle.html Barrow sign up Nickle] The Telegraph, 29 November 2001</ref><ref>[http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_league/1682933.stm Tollett quits Broncos] BBC Sport, 29 November 2001</ref> He then moved into the media and now presents sport on [[BBC News (British TV channel)|BBC News]] in the UK and [[BBC News (international TV channel)|internationally]]. Previous to this he worked for [[ABC News (Australian TV channel)|ABC News]] and [[ABC NewsRadio]] in Sydney and has also worked for [[BBC Radio 5 Live]],<ref name=TakeToll/> [[Sky Sports]], [[Eurosport]] and [[Setanta Sports]] amongst others as a presenter, reporter or commentator, and worked as a pitchside reporter for TVH, the host broadcaster at the [[2007 Rugby World Cup]] in France.

In 2021, Tollett was a commentator for [[Channel 4]]'s coverage of the [[2020 Paralympics]].

==Qualifications==
Tollett holds a Bachelor of Physical Education teaching degree, as well as a first dan karate black belt.
